Why Can't Ordinary People Access Policy Dividends?

Can't Find It

faq.q1_desc: A One-Person Company (OPC) is the hottest entrepreneurial trend of 2026. One person, empowered by AI tools and automation systems, independently executes what traditionally required a full team. From freelancing and side hustles to full-time entrepreneurship, OPC is becoming the choice of millions. Covered by People's Daily and 36Kr.
faq.q2_desc: Not everyone is cut out for independent business. The OPC Assessment evaluates your fitness across 6 dimensions — Decision-Making, Resource Integration, Risk Resilience, Brand Building, Systems Operations, Continuous Learning. Complete the test in 15 minutes and receive your personalized assessment report and action roadmap. Over 1,000 people have used it to upgrade their self-awareness.
faq.q2_title: Am I Suited to Be a One-Person Company?
faq.q3_desc: OPC businesses span a wide range: AI content creation, personal branding, digital products, e-commerce dropshipping, social media management, consulting, and more. Based on your capability profile, OPC recommends your top 3 business tracks with a 30-day actionable SOP plan.
faq.q3_title: What Businesses Can an OPC Run?
faq.title: What You Need to Know About OPC

glossary.SOP_def: Standard Operating Procedure — streamlining repetitive work into repeatable processes for improved execution efficiency.
glossary.biz_loop_def: The complete monetization chain from customer acquisition, sales, delivery, to after-sales. The more complete the loop, the more stable the income.
glossary.core_gift_def: Your individually verified capability with differentiated market advantages — the foundational competitive moat of a one-person company.
glossary.custom_track_def: Business direction and launch plan tailored to your capability profile and current stage.
glossary.opc_def: A small-scale business centered around an individual or minimal team, pursuing high profit, strong business loops, risk resilience, and absolute career sovereignty.
glossary.productize_def: Transforming personal experience, skills, or services into standardized, sellable product formats — the leap from 'selling time' to 'selling products.'

grade.B: B · Solid Foundation, Ready to Break Through

privacy.title: Privacy Policy — OPC Incubator
privacy.content:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: May 12, 2026

1. What Information We Collect

When you use the OPC Incubator ("the System"), we may collect the following information:

  • Basic Profile Information: Age range, gender, occupation status, education level, referral source (submitted only on profile.html).
  • Assessment Responses: Your answers and scores for the 15 assessment questions.
  • Phone Number: Collected only when you actively choose "Send full report to my phone" on the report page.
  • Automatically Collected Data: Anonymous visit data via Baidu Analytics (page views, time spent, referral source, etc.), containing no personal identity information.
  • Cookies: Used for language preference storage, visit deduplication, and other basic functions.

2. How We Use Information

  • Generate your OPC fitness assessment report.
  • Send the full report via text message (only when explicitly requested).
  • Optimize the question matching algorithm to improve assessment accuracy.
  • Analyze website usage to improve user experience.

3. Data Storage and Protection

Your assessment data and profile information are stored in browser local storage (localStorage) and are only accessible on your current device. We do not upload assessment data to cloud servers. Phone numbers are stored locally and only used when operators export data; they are not automatically uploaded.

4. Information Sharing and Disclosure

We do not sell, rent, or share your personal information with any third party, except:

  • When disclosure is required by laws and regulations.
  • When you explicitly authorize us to share (e.g., sharing necessary information with service agents for subsidy applications, requiring your separate confirmation).

5. Third-Party Services

The System uses the following third-party services, which may independently collect information:

  • Baidu Analytics (hm.baidu.com): Anonymous analysis of website traffic and user behavior.

These services' privacy policies are beyond our control. We recommend reviewing their respective privacy terms.

6. Your Rights

You have the right to clear your browser cache and localStorage data at any time to delete assessment records and preferences. To confirm whether a submitted phone number has been deleted, please contact us via the methods below.

7. Policy Updates

We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Updated policies will be posted on this page with the revision date noted at the top.

8. Contact

If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us via:

WeChat customer service (see bottom of each page)

report_paid.cta_body: Book a 1-on-1 deep diagnosis, with a senior coach tailoring the report analysis and landing plan for you.
report_paid.pitfall_1: Don't wait until everything is ready — launch first, iterate later. Perfectionism is the #1 killer of momentum.
report_paid.pitfall_2: Don't try to do everything at once — focus on one track, one product, one channel. Go deep before going wide.
report_paid.pitfall_3: Don't ignore cash flow management — even a small business needs clear bookkeeping and pricing.
report_paid.pitfall_4: Don't work in isolation — actively join communities, find a mentor or partner. Solo doesn't mean alone.
report_paid.pitfall_5: Don't neglect your health — you ARE the company's core asset. Maintaining energy = maintaining competitiveness.
report_paid.pitfall_subtitle: 5 most common traps on the solo-business journey

registration.cta_desc: Whether you want to DIY with guidance or fully delegate — start with a fitness assessment and get your personalized registration plan.
registration.desc: OPC Incubator provides business registration services for sole proprietorships and one-person limited liability companies. Covering 20 cities nationwide, license in as fast as half a day, subsidy application handled end-to-end.
registration.hero_desc: 20 Cities Nationwide · License in Half a Day · One-Stop Subsidy Application
registration.req1_desc: Chinese citizens with full civil capacity, or foreign nationals with valid residence permits, can register.
registration.req2_desc: A commercial or office address is required. Most cities support virtual or affiliated address registration, costing as little as ¥500/year.
registration.req3_desc: Most industries can register as an OPC. Special industries (finance, healthcare, education, etc.) require pre-approval — we'll help you confirm.
registration.req4_desc: The legal representative must not be on the list of dishonest judgment debtors. We help screen this in advance.
registration.step1_desc: Gather the required registration documents. Requirements vary slightly by city and registration type — we'll help you check each one.
registration.step1_li2: Registered address proof (lease or virtual address)
registration.step2_desc: Submit the registration application to the local Market Regulation Bureau. We handle the entire process on your behalf, including follow-ups and corrections.
registration.step2_li3: Market Regulation Bureau review (5–10 business days)
registration.step3_desc: After receiving your license, open a corporate bank account and complete tax registration. This is where many entrepreneurs get stuck — we provide full guidance.
registration.title: OPC Business Registration — Sole Proprietorship / OPC License | OPC Incubator

services.subsidy_desc: Average entrepreneur qualifies for ¥6,000-35,000 in subsidies. We find them, handle the paperwork, and get you paid.
services.subsidy_flow_desc: Submit your info → We assess eligibility → Confirm service plan → Local agent takes over → Subsidy paid
services.subsidy_or: Or take the free assessment first to understand your OPC fitness
services.subsidy_refund: * Full refund if unsuccessful. No charge if subsidy amount is less than service fee.
services.subsidy_what1: Match all eligible subsidies based on your city and profile
services.subsidy_what2: Prepare application materials (business license, social insurance records, forms)
services.subsidy_what3: Connect with local agents and track progress end-to-end
services.subsidy_what4: Ensure subsidies are paid on time
services.subsidy_who1: Just registered an individual business / OPC, first time applying
services.subsidy_who2: Not sure what subsidies are available in your city
services.subsidy_who3: No time for paperwork, want an agent to handle it
services.subsidy_who4: Want to check if there are unclaimed subsidies
services.tax_bookkeeping_desc1: Sole proprietors can choose "deemed levy" or "audit levy":
services.tax_bookkeeping_note: Most OPC startups choose deemed levy — simplest paperwork.
services.tax_bookkeeping_type1_desc: Tax bureau sets a deemed profit rate based on industry average. Actual costs don't matter. Best for micro businesses.
services.tax_bookkeeping_type2_desc: Requires proper records. Profit = actual revenue minus actual costs. For larger operations.
services.tax_calc1_note: Income tax alone saves over ¥10,000. Add VAT exemption and other tax cuts for even more savings.
services.tax_desc: China offers significant tax breaks for sole proprietors. No accountant needed — here's everything you need to know.
services.tax_faq_a1: A: Yes. Even with zero income, you must file a "zero declaration" or risk being listed as non-compliant.
services.tax_faq_a2: A: Absolutely. The e-tax bureau handles everything online. If it's too much hassle, bookkeeping services cost ~¥200-500/month.
services.tax_faq_a3: A: Fines of ¥200-2,000. Three consecutive months of non-filing can get you blacklisted.
services.tax_faq_q1: Q: Do I need to file with zero income?
services.tax_faq_q3: Q: What if I forget to file?
services.tax_monthly_desc: Most sole proprietors file quarterly (Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct). Log into the e-tax bureau (etax.chinatax.gov.cn), takes just a few minutes.
services.tax_policy1_desc: Monthly revenue under ¥100,000 (quarterly ¥300,000) — zero VAT. Automatically applied when filing.
services.tax_policy2_desc: Individual income tax on business profits is halved for annual taxable income up to ¥2,000,000.
services.tax_policy2_title: ② Income Tax: 50% Off Under ¥2M/Year Profit
services.tax_policy3_desc: Urban construction tax, stamp duty, property tax, and more — all reduced by 50%.
services.tax_policy3_title: ③ Six Taxes + Two Fees: All Halved
services.tax_title: OPC Tax Filing: Easier Than You Think
services.tax_yearly_desc: January 1 – March 31 each year: calculate total annual income minus expenses to determine actual profit and final income tax.

theory.book2_desc: Redefining success: right-sized, profit-first, lifestyle-first. Our Systems Operations and Risk Resilience dimensions draw directly from this framework.
theory.book3_desc: A model for capability migration from organizational person to free agent. Core focus on brand building, resource integration, and continuous learning — the practical guide to solo-business capability.
theory.subtitle: Three landmark works form the intellectual backbone of this assessment
theory.title: Theoretical Foundation

The report highlighted 'Content Ability' and 'Continuous Operations' as her hidden strengths, suggesting a 'Personal Brand + Consulting' path. She followed the 30-day SOP and landed her first paid consulting client within a month.

cases.title: OPC Case Files — 3 Real Stories | OPC One-Person Company Incubator
cases.meta_desc: Three real OPC incubation cases: a Shenzhen renovation foreman's subsidy breakthrough, a Shanghai UI designer's turnaround, and a Chengdu solopreneur coach's failure. Real data, no filters.
cases.keywords: OPC cases,solopreneur stories,startup cases,one-person business,solopreneur failure
cases.og_desc: 2 success stories + 1 failure lesson. Real data, no filters. See which path is closest to yours.
cases.hero_sub: 2 success stories + 1 failure lesson.Real data, no filters. See which path is closest to yours.
cases.case1_profile: Lao Zhou, 38 · Interior Renovation Management · Shenzhen
cases.case1_bg_text1: 12 years as a renovation foreman in Shenzhen, always operating under someone else's company license. At the end of 2025, the license holder took a 15% cut and delayed payments. In January 2026, he registered his own sole proprietorship — but had no business. Three potential clients were in negotiation but none converted.
cases.case1_bg_text2: Market data: According to Shenzhen Housing Authority's 2025 report, ~4,200 registered renovation sole proprietors in the city. Less than 15% earn ¥50-80K monthly on average; most earn under ¥20K. 90% of renovation foremen operate under a company license, which takes 10-20%.
cases.case1_int_1: Assessment profile — Extremely high execution ability (89%), but channels entirely reliant on old client referrals. Zero online acquisition capability.
cases.case1_int_2: Channel strategy — Skip Xiaohongshu/Douyin. Go WeChat private domain + property management partnerships — 90% of renovation clients come from referrals and property manager recommendations.
cases.case1_int_3: Subsidy application — Shenzhen sole proprietor social insurance subsidy (¥890/mo) + startup rental subsidy (¥1,560/mo), totaling ¥29,400/year. Lao Zhou didn't know these existed.
cases.case1_int_4: Client acquisition — Built a WeChat business card page (name + credentials + completed project photos + client review QR code) to replace traditional paper cards.
cases.case1_result_1: Subsidy: First application approved March 2026, first payment of ¥2,450 arrived in April
cases.case1_result_2: Business: Through property partnerships (10% referral fee), signed 6 new deals in 3 months, totaling ¥180K
cases.case1_quote: "The subsidy money wasn't the big part, but it made me realize — these things can be done systematically. Before, I just worked with my head down, living day to day."
cases.case1_time: OPC time invested: 3 hrs material prep + 2 hrs property negotiation script = 5 hrs. Lao Zhou runs it himself from there.
cases.case2_title: Shanghai UI Designer's Content Model Turnaround
cases.case2_profile: Xiao Lin, 27 · AI Product UI/UX Design · Shanghai
cases.case2_bg_text1: UI designer at a Shanghai internet company, laid off at the end of 2025. Severance N+1, ¥120K in savings. Wanted to freelance UI design but sent 30+ portfolios across platforms and only got 2 inquiries — neither converted. Savings burning, scrolling Boss Zhipin and ZBJ every day.
cases.case2_bg_text2: Market data: Q1 2026, 1,200+ new AI startups registered in China (Qichacha). 75% are looking for UI design. Agencies charge ¥50K+ — beyond their budget. Solo UI designers slot right in.
cases.case2_int_1: Assessment profile — Exceptional professional skills (92%), but zero client acquisition and sales ability (23%). Classic craftsman mindset.
cases.case2_int_2: Positioning — Skip ZBJ low-ball gigs. Position: AI product UI design (hottest niche for 2026). Deal size starting at ¥3,500.
cases.case2_int_3: Content model — Topic direction: analyze one AI product UI problem per day (Xiaohongshu + Bilibili). Output cadence: 3 breakdowns + 1 workflow + 1 resource per week. Conversion path: comments → DMs → portfolio PDF → call → proposal.
cases.case2_int_4: Pricing strategy — New client trial at full price but with free icon set; 10% off for repeat; 20% off for monthly retainer.
cases.case2_int_5: AI toolchain — Taught Midjourney + Claude for design drafts, cutting 3 days to 6 hours.
cases.case2_result_1: Month 1: 0 deals (content account building phase — normal)
cases.case2_result_2: Month 2: 8 DM inquiries, 3 deals closed, avg ¥3,500 each
cases.case2_result_3: Month 3: 12 inquiries, 6 deals closed, signed one AI startup on monthly retainer ¥12,000/mo
cases.case2_result_4: Monthly income grew from ¥0 to steady ¥15,000-20,000
cases.case2_time: OPC time invested: 3 hrs content model design + 1 hr pricing strategy + monthly reviews (30 min each) = ~6 hrs upfront + 2 hrs/month follow-up.
cases.case3_title: He Wanted to Be a "Solopreneur Coach" — No One Paid
cases.case3_bg_text1: Chengdu. Previously did community operations at a paid knowledge membership group. Quit in early 2026 to become a "solopreneur coach" — teaching others how to run one-person businesses. 80 followers on Zhihu, 200 WeChat contacts, no product. Wanted to charge ¥9,999 for "personal brand coaching."
cases.case3_bg_text2: Market data: China's knowledge-payment market was ¥220B in 2025. "Solopreneur + coach" has 32,000 posts on Xiaohongshu. 90% of "solopreneur coaches" have never actually run a successful one-person business themselves.
cases.case3_tl_1: Month 0: Quit job, posted 3 WeChat Moments: "Coaching ¥9,999/person." Responses: 0
cases.case3_tl_2: Month 1: Posted on Zhihu "Top 10 Solopreneur Mistakes." 37 views. Posted 3 articles then stopped.
cases.case3_tl_3: Month 2: Started selling ¥9.9 "startup resource pack" (public info scraped from the web). Sold 17 copies, earned ¥168. Two people publicly called him a "韭菜收割机" (hustler preying on newbies).
cases.case3_tl_5: Month 4: Canceled business license, started sending resumes
cases.case3_tl_6: Month 5: Back to old career doing community operations, ¥8,000/month salary. Never mentioned the word "startup" again.
cases.case3_analysis_title: Failure Analysis: Why Couldn't OPC Stop This?
cases.case3_row1_reality: Extremely saturated supply. 90% of coaches never succeeded themselves.
cases.case3_row3_reality: 0 followers, 0 products, 0 credentials. Even ¥9.9 was too much.
cases.case3_row4_reality: Posted 3 Zhihu articles, stopped. 1 WeChat Moment, stopped.
cases.case3_row5_reality: Only listened to what he wanted to hear. Did nothing he didn't want to hear.
cases.case3_quote: "It wasn't your problem. It was mine. I thought knowing how to do it meant I could do it. Turns out, the gap between knowing and doing is a f***ing ocean."
cases.lesson_1: Channel choice beats hard work. Lao Zhou picked the right acquisition path (property partnerships). Xiao Lin picked the right niche (AI product UI). A Qiang picked a niche he wasn't qualified for.
cases.lesson_2: Cash reserves are oxygen. ¥30K for 3 months is almost certainly not enough. OPC recommends: save at least 6 months of living expenses before starting.
cases.lesson_3: Tools can be given, execution cannot. Same content model — Xiao Lin executed, A Qiang stopped after one post.
cases.lesson_4: Do first, then teach. A "coach" with zero hands-on experience is building castles in the air. If you want to do education/consulting — run a real business first.
cases.lesson_5: OPC doesn't take people without a product/service foundation. A Qiang's lesson clarified our boundaries: not everyone is suited for independent business. Take the assessment first, then decide.
cases.cta_desc: 15 minutes to see how close you are to running a one-person company

services_process.title: Solopreneur Timeline Comparison — Three Tracks | OPC Incubator
services_process.meta_desc: Real timeline comparison across three tracks: renovation foreman, AI product designer, and a failed solopreneur coach. See how long your solo journey will take. Pick the right track, avoid the wrong turns.
services_process.keywords: solopreneur track,startup timeline,solo business process,one-person company comparison,OPC tracks
services_process.og_title: Solopreneur Timeline Comparison — Three Tracks | OPC Incubator
services_process.og_desc: Your track. How long will it take to run it alone? Three real timelines tell the story.
services_process.hero_sub: Pick the track closest to your situation, follow the timeline. If you can clearly say "I'll get stuck at month X doing Y" — you can probably run it yourself. If you can only say "this looks complicated" — that's why OPC exists.
services_process.track1_sub: Manual labor / low formal education · Shenzhen · Eligible subsidies: social insurance ¥890/mo + rental ¥1,560/mo
services_process.t1_m1_text: Quit job / leave umbrella company → lose stable income, anxiety kicks in. Register sole proprietorship (visit business bureau / get seal / open bank account) → 7-14 days, don't know where to start, materials rejected. Find work (WeChat Moments / property offices) → no response, getting rejected. Income: ¥0
services_process.t1_m2_text: Visit 10 property offices for partnerships → 9 rejections. First inquiry → no idea how to price. First deal signed ¥30K → don't know how to write a contract. Work starts → supplier cheats on materials, workers demand more pay. Final payment → client nitpicks and delays. Income: ¥30K (gross profit ¥9,000)
services_process.t1_m3_text: First deal final payment delayed 10 days. Discover startup subsidies → Shenzhen conditions complex, can't understand. First subsidy submission → rejected. Fix and resubmit → waiting. Income: ¥30K (gross profit ¥7,000)
services_process.t1_m4_text: Averaging 1-2 deals/month, ¥30-50K revenue. Can't figure out the accounting. Subsidy finally arrives ¥2,450. Afraid to quit, not sure if next month will have deals. 4-month net income: ~¥26K
services_process.track2_sub: Skilled + young · Shanghai · Eligible subsidy: flexible employment social insurance ¥600/mo
services_process.t2_m1_text: Laid off / quit → cash flow anxiety. Register sole proprietorship (online) → 3 days. Prepare portfolio → not sure what to include. Register on all platforms → send 30 DMs/portfolios → 2 responses. First gig ¥800 (ZBJ) → 3 days of work, hourly rate ¥110. Income: ¥800
services_process.t2_m2_text: Keep posting content, no views. Second gig ¥1,500 (friend referral) → friend haggles price. Check Shanghai subsidy → conditions complex, don't want to deal with it. Pay social insurance ¥2,000/mo. Income: ¥2,300 | Expenses: ¥3,500 | Net loss: ¥1,200
services_process.t2_m3_text: Savings ¥120K→¥95K. Girlfriend pushes to find a job. Scroll Boss Zhipin every day, UI competition ratio 1:47. Consider lowering prices to win deals but feels it's not worth it. End-of-month state: debating whether to go back to a 9-to-5
services_process.t2_i1_opc: AI product UI topic library (30 directions)
services_process.t2_i3_opc: Pricing strategy (new ¥3K-5K / repeat 10% off / monthly 20% off)
services_process.t2_i4_user: Go apply at social insurance bureau
services_process.track3_sub: Chengdu · Knowledge-pay track · ¥30K savings, gave up after 5 months
services_process.t3_m0_text: 6 months as community ops at a paid knowledge group. Thinks they've figured it out. Quits to become "solopreneur coach." Posts 3 WeChat Moments: "Coaching ¥9,999/person." Responses: 0
services_process.t3_m1_text: Posts on Zhihu "Top 10 Solopreneur Mistakes." 37 views. 3 posts, stops posting. Considers dropping price to ¥999 to get first client. OPC advice: "Run a real business first, then teach." — They don't buy it.
services_process.t3_m2_text: Savings ¥30K→¥15K. Starts selling ¥9.9 "startup resource pack." Sells 17 copies, ¥168 revenue. 2 people publicly call them a scammer. Starts losing sleep.
services_process.t3_m3_5_text: Borrows ¥12,000 online loans. Cancels business license. Sends resumes. Back to old career doing community ops ¥8,000/mo. Never mentions the word "startup" again.
services_process.track3_note: The lesson of this case: don't teach what you haven't done. The gap between knowing and doing is an ocean.
services_process.cta_desc: Picking the right track beats pure effort. Pick the wrong one and you end up like A Qiang — 5 months in, realizing the direction was never right. Test first, see the timeline, then decide whether to go for it.

services_tax.ld_article: { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Article", "name": "Tax Basics for Sole Proprietors", "description": "The simplest tax filing guide for sole proprietors and one-person companies: VAT exemption, 50% income tax cut, six taxes + two fees halved.", "author": { "@type": "Organization", "name": "OPC One-Person Company Incubator" } }
services_tax.hero_title: Tax Filing IsSimpler Than You Think
services_tax.hero_sub: China offers substantial tax benefits for small businesses. No accountant needed, no bookkeeping software required. Read on and you'll understand.
services_tax.policy1_desc: Monthly sales revenue under ¥100K (quarterly ¥300K)? Zero VAT. No application needed — automatically waived when you file.
services_tax.policy2_title: Personal Income Tax: 50% Off on Annual Income ≤ ¥2M
services_tax.policy2_desc: For sole proprietors, taxable profit (revenue minus costs) up to ¥2M/year gets a 50% income tax reduction.
services_tax.policy3_title: Six Taxes + Two Fees: All Halved
services_tax.policy3_desc: Urban maintenance tax, stamp duty, property tax, urban land use tax, farmland occupation tax, resource tax + education surcharge and local education surcharge — all cut by 50%.
services_tax.calc1_note: That's over ¥10K saved on income tax alone. Add VAT exemption and the six taxes + two fees halving, and the savings are even bigger.
services_tax.when_heading: When Do You Need to File Taxes?
services_tax.quarterly_desc: Most sole proprietors file quarterly (January, April, July, October). Log into the e-tax bureau (etax.chinatax.gov.cn) — it takes just a few minutes.
services_tax.annual_desc: Every year, January 1 to March 31, file the annual reconciliation for the previous year's business income. Calculate total annual revenue minus total costs to determine actual profit, then compute the tax owed.
services_tax.bookkeeping_heading: Do You Need to Keep Books?
services_tax.bookkeeping_intro: Sole proprietors can choose between two methods:
services_tax.bookkeeping_1: Deemed Assessment: Tax bureau sets a "deemed profit rate" based on your industry average. Actual costs don't matter. Best for micro sole proprietors.
services_tax.bookkeeping_2: Audited Assessment: Requires accounting records. Tax is calculated on actual revenue minus actual costs. Suitable for larger-scale operations.
services_tax.bookkeeping_note: Most one-person companies in the startup phase choose deemed assessment — the simplest process.
services_tax.faq1_q: Q: Do I need to file if I have no income?
services_tax.faq1_a: A: Yes. Even with zero income, you must file a "zero return" or risk being flagged as a non-operating entity.
services_tax.faq2_a: A: Absolutely. The e-tax bureau handles everything online. If it feels like too much, accounting firms charge about ¥200-500/month.
services_tax.faq3_q: Q: What happens if I forget to file?
services_tax.faq3_a: A: Fines range from ¥200-2,000. Missing 3 consecutive months puts you on the business abnormality list.
services_tax.footer_note: General tax information. Consult your local tax bureau for specifics.

subsidy_checklist.title: 20-City Startup Subsidy Checklist — What Sole Proprietors Can Claim | OPC Incubator
subsidy_checklist.meta_desc: A comprehensive table of startup subsidy amounts and conditions across 20 Chinese cities. Side-by-side comparison for sole proprietors and one-person companies. Up to ¥60K+ available.
subsidy_checklist.keywords: startup subsidy checklist,city subsidy comparison,sole proprietor subsidy amounts,20-city subsidy overview,OPC subsidies
subsidy_checklist.og_desc: One table to see all startup subsidy amounts and conditions across 20 cities. Find your city and see how much you can claim.
subsidy_checklist.hero_sub: Find your city and see exactly how much you can claim. Amounts and conditions updated annually — below is the latest 2026 data.
subsidy_checklist.note_1: Amounts above are reference ranges. Refer to the latest local government announcements for exact figures.
subsidy_checklist.note_2: "One-time startup grants" in most cities require local household registration or graduation within 5 years.
subsidy_checklist.note_3: Social insurance subsidies require active business operations + social insurance contributions (flexible employment insurance typically excluded).
subsidy_checklist.note_4: Rental subsidies require a physical business premises lease. Virtual addresses generally don't qualify.
subsidy_checklist.note_5: Startup guaranteed loans require counter-guarantees or credit history. Interest rates are typically at the benchmark rate.
subsidy_checklist.note_6: First-time subsidy application rejection rate is ~62% — consider using an agent or carefully reviewing materials.
subsidy_checklist.note_7: Click any city name to view detailed policy breakdown and application procedures.
subsidy_checklist.cta_title: Not Sure Which Subsidies You Qualify For?
subsidy_checklist.cta_desc: OPC matches all available subsidies based on your city and personal situation, helps prepare materials, and follows through until funds arrive. Full refund if unsuccessful.
subsidy_checklist.footer_note: Data sources: city-level human resources and social security bureau, market supervision administration websites. Policies may change; refer to latest local announcements.
